rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Certain Requests For Admission Against Plaintiffs Be Deemed Admitted; And Request For Mandatory Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendants McDonnell Douglas Corporation and The Boeing Company¿s motion for an order that the truth of the matters specified in certain requests for admission be deemed admitted and for sanctions is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LIMA VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff¿S Notice Of Motion To Compel Verified Discovery Responses To Defendant John K. Bice Company Inc., Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel verified responses to written discovery from defendant John K. Bice Company, Inc. and motion to deem requests for admission admitted are denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to provide verifications. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Since defendant is not required to provide verifications, defendant¿s timely answers to plaintiff¿s requests for admission will not be deemed admitted. Both plaintiff¿s and defendant¿s requests for monetary sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR BONETTO, ET AL VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Pltfs' Objections To Its Case-Specific Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant C&M Building Materials, Inc.¿s motion to strike plaintiffs¿ objections to its case-specific discovery requests is denied. Plaintiffs served on defendant their responses, signed by plaintiffs¿ counsel, within the time limit prescribed by the Code. Plaintiffs did not waive their objections because verifications are not required to preserve objections. (See C.C.P. §§ 2030.250, 2031.250 , 2033.240; Food 4 Less Supermarkets, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (Fletcher) (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 651, 657.) Plaintiffs¿ request for monetary sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR BONETTO, ET AL VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Further Deposition And Request For Production Of Documents Of Deft Westside Building Material Corporation'S Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. The hearing will be at 9:45am. The parties should be prepared to apprise the court of the status of the stipulation discussed at the November 14 hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM M. MCRAY et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Motion To Quash Videotaped Deposition Of William Mcray, Sr., And Request For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, December 20, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s joinder in defendants¿ motion to quash plaintiff¿s notice of taking videotaped deposition of William McRay, Sr. is denied. Defendant¿s motion for protective order is granted in part and denied in part. As to the motion to quash, defendants failed to demonstrate that they served written objections to the deposition notice so they waived any error or irregularity in the notice. (C.C.P. 2025.410(a).) As to the motion for protective order, defendants¿ motion is granted in that the deposition must not occur on Saturdays, Sundays or holidays. The motion is denied in that plaintiff does not have to provide defendants with the requested information (ship lists, auto lists, etc.) because defendants cited no authority that requires such information be produced. The motion is further denied in that the deposition will take place at the location specified in plaintiff¿s amended notice. It will not take place in the Bay Area. (See C.C.P. 2025.250(a).) The stay of the deposition is lifted. Defendants¿ and plaintiff¿s requests for monetary sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
